sections.about: Turnover captures the scale of operating revenue disclosed under BRSR Section A — General Disclosures, primarily through products/services sold and CSR financial details. It is not a Principle 1–9 Essential or Leadership indicator; it is a Section A business profile disclosure. A higher value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: On a normalised INR crore basis, Tata Steel moved from ~₹129,006.6 crore in FY2022-23 to ~₹140,987.4 crore in FY2023-24, before moderating to ₹132,516.7 crore in FY2024-25; the FY2024-25 Tata BRSR shows standalone turnover of ₹132,516.66 crore and consolidated turnover of ₹218,542.51 crore, with steel products contributing 93% of standalone turnover. JSW stayed close to Tata at ₹130,039.0 crore, ₹133,609.0 crore, and ₹125,678.0 crore across the three years, reflecting a large flat/long carbon steel platform but below Tata in FY2024 and FY2025. SAIL was structurally lower but stable at ~₹104,447.4 crore in FY2022-23 and ~₹104,545.1 crore in FY2023-24, then declined to ₹101,716.0 crore in FY2024-25; its FY2022-23 annual report attributes the operating environment to global energy crisis, inflationary pressure, supply-chain disruption, interest-rate hikes, and steel-price volatility, while domestic demand was supported by infrastructure and manufacturing spend. Jindal Stainless increased consistently from ~₹34,849.0 crore to ~₹38,356.0 crore and ~₹40,816.9 crore, but remains much smaller in absolute turnover because it is a stainless-focused, more specialised portfolio rather than a broad integrated carbon-steel platform.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata Steel ranked 1st on turnover. JSW was closest at ₹125,678.0 crore, around 5.2% below Tata’s ₹132,516.7 crore. Tata’s rank improved from 2nd in FY2022-23 to 1st in FY2023-24 and stayed 1st in FY2024-25, despite the decline in absolute turnover from FY2023-24. The rank improvement was driven by Tata retaining a marginally larger standalone revenue base than JSW and a broader business mix, while SAIL remained behind due to lower turnover scale and Jindal Stainless remained structurally smaller despite its strong growth trajectory.
sections.comparabilityNotes: Rankings use INR crore-normalised values for consistency. Tata Steel FY2022-23 is consolidated and therefore includes international operations, whereas the other company-years are standalone; Tata’s FY2024-25 BRSR also notes standalone and consolidated turnover separately.
